mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49% on 2007-11-01 reflects Fed easing cycle initiated in September 2007
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% signal labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when unemployment crosses above the 5.0% barrier.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% on 2007-11-01, approaching historical trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly decline, signaling demand-side contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations. The interaction between weakening consumer spending and residential investment creates a multiplicative negative effect on equity valuations over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1229k units (2007-10-01 vintage) reflects housing market weakness
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal reduced manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness, indicating deteriorating corporate profitability. This mechanism operates continuously, with stronger negative effects during periods of already-low capacity utilization.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9019 (2007-11-01 vintage) shows recent weakness from prior peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) create a dual labor market shock that signals imminent earnings recession. The interaction between job losses and rising joblessness amplifies negative equity sentiment over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138467k (2007-11-01 vintage) near recent peaks with UNRATE at 4.7%
failure_condition: Simultaneous PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while Federal funds rate remains below 5.0%, creating a real rate compression that signals monetary policy lag. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative equity returns as the Fed faces pressure to tighten, raising future discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 210.565 (2007-11-01 vintage) reflects elevated inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Real rate compression episodes do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term (inflation × low rates) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declines below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when quarterly GDP growth falls below the 2.0% threshold, predicting negative 60-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11659.3B (2007-07-01 vintage) reflects Q3 2007 economic activity
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth < 2.0% do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1200k units annualized rate, signaling residential investment weakness that cascades through construction employment and consumer wealth effects. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day equity returns as housing-sensitive sectors face earnings headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1229k units (2007-10-01 vintage) near critical threshold
failure_condition: HOUST < 1200k does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during housing downturns.
